title: ENGINE REMOVAL — Removal (Cont'd) / Installation

figures_detected.description: Power steering pump on engine, showing location for removal/tie-off to vehicle body
Air conditioning compressor location on engine, showing fixing bolts for removal/tie-off to vehicle body
Engine slinger brackets attached to engine, with crane slingers in position
Centremember mounting bolt locations on vehicle body underside
Diagram of engine mounting bracket showing clearance dimensions: 10 mm (0.39 in) on all four sides

steps.text: Remove engine harness from harness saddle on engine mounting.
Remove main earth lead from body.
On power steering equipped models, slacken water pump drive belt. Refer to section MA. Loosen and remove power steering pump fixing bolts. Tie power steering pump onto vehicle body.
Disconnect throttle cable. Disconnect kickdown cable (NCVT).
Disconnect brake vacuum hose from brake servo.

On air conditioning equipped models, slacken alternator drive belt. Refer to section MA. Remove alternator. Loosen and remove air conditioning compressor fixing bolts. Tie air conditioning compressor onto vehicle body.
Attach engine slinger brackets to engine. Engine slinger brackets and engine slinger bracket bolts are described in PARTS CATALOG.
Remove 4 centermember mounting bolts from vehicle body.
Attach slingers to engine slinger brackets and use crane to release load from both top engine mountings.
Lower engine onto transmission jack or floor.
Install top engine mounting brackets and fixing bolts.
Carefully move engine onto engine mounting insulators.

content_blocks.text: Ensure that insulators are positioned on brackets correctly.

model_notes: Steps 32 and 38 apply to power steering equipped and air conditioning equipped models respectively. Step 34 references kickdown cable for NCVT (CVT) models.
